danger_signals.visual_form: Bright colored bullets/energy bolts contrasting with background
Distinct sprite designs for hostile aircraft and ground units
Large, detailed sprite with multiple attack patterns and visual flourishes
danger_signals.meaning: Significant threat escalation requiring focused engagement

danger_signals.visual_form: Expansive projectile patterns or energy waves filling play area
danger_signals.meaning: Critical evasion required; high danger state

safety_signals.visual_form: Absence of enemy sprites and projectiles in immediate vicinity
Glowing collectible items dropped by defeated enemies
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary advantage or resource gain available

safety_signals.visual_form: Distinctive visual pickups (likely cross or plus symbols)

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Player-controlled aircraft sprite at screen bottom; positioned centrally or per input

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple distinct enemy aircraft types with varying sprite sizes and attack patterns; ground-based turrets and installations
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Enemy sprites appear from top of screen or sides; concentrated in specific stage zones

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Sparse enemy placement; smaller projectile counts; cleaner screen composition
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies follow predictable patrol patterns; attack with limited frequency

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Increased enemy sprite density; more complex bullet patterns; visual clutter increases
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy formations tighten; attack frequency and projectile volume increase; coordinated multi-unit patterns emerge

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Boss sprite dominates screen center or significant portion; elaborate attack visualization with multiple projectile types; background may shift or intensify
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss executes multi-phase attack patterns; screen-filling projectile waves; vulnerable phases visually distinct

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Screen clears; brief respite with minimal enemies; visual staging suggests progression or location change
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy pressure momentarily reduces; allows player recovery and repositioning

environmental_storytelling.example: Different aircraft and ground installations appear in specific stage zones, implying different military installations or defensive positions
Boss attack patterns increase in elaboration and screen-filling capacity, communicating escalating threat and enemy adaptation
Visual crowding increases across stage progression, indicating player advancing into defended territory

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Follows predetermined flight path; maintains distance until player enters range
Fires projectiles in patterns; may attempt evasion or aggressive positioning
Stationary; sprite visible but not firing
Fires upward projectile streams when player approaches
Executes primary attack pattern; limited vulnerable windows
Attack patterns intensify; screen-filling projectile waves; behavior becomes more erratic or multi-directional

notes: Strike Gunner S.T.G. is a vertically-scrolling shoot-em-up with military aesthetic. World state is communicated primarily through enemy deployment density, projectile complexity, and sprite scale/visual weight rather than explicit environmental changes. Danger escalation is linear and progression-based. No faction politics or ideological visual markers present; conflict is purely oppositional (player vs. undifferentiated enemy forces). The game lacks narrative or faction differentiation—all visual communication serves mechanical difficulty and player threat assessment.
